appavattaṁ.meaning: non-occurrence; standstill, stagnancy; not rolling on
appavattaṁ.summary: This is a negated participle meaning “not rolling on”, i.e. coming to a standstill.
breakdown: Negative prefix meaning “not” (with sandhi doubling of the following p).
Rolled on, occurring; from pa + √vatt “to turn, proceed”.
Negative prefix meaning “not” (with sandhi doubling of the following p).
Negative prefix meaning “not” (with sandhi doubling of the following p).
Penetration, comprehension; piercing through to the truth; from paṭi + √vidh “to pierce”.

appaṭivijjhanto.gram: present participle, nominative singular masculine, of na paṭivijjhati
breakdown: Negative prefix meaning “not” (with sandhi doubling of the following p).
Piercing; present participle of vijjhati, from √vidh “to pierce”.

appesakkhasaṁvattanikā.meaning: conducive to little influence; leading to little power
appesakkhasaṁvattanikā.summary: This is a compound meaning “conducive to having little influence”.
breakdown: Of little influence, of little power (appa “little” + esakkha).
Conducive to, leading to; from saṁ + √vatt.

appesakkhasaṁvat­.meaning: conducive to little influence; leading to little power
appesakkhasaṁvat­.note: uncertain: this headword is a line-break fragment of appesakkhasaṁvattanikā in the source vocabulary; see that entry.

appesakkho.meaning: of little influence; of little power
breakdown: Influence, standing; the lexicon parses the compound literally as “few friends”.
appesakkho.note: divergence: the lexicon parses literally as appa + sakkha (“few + friends”); DPD derives the compound from appa + yasas + ka (“little fame/influence”). Both yield the sense “of little influence”.

appicchatā.meaning: fewness of wishes; contentedness; having few wants

breakdown: Negative prefix meaning “not” (with sandhi doubling of the following p).

appiyehi.meaning: with those who are disagreeable; with the unloved
appiyehi.gram: adjective, instrumental/ablative plural of appiya (na + piya)
breakdown: Negative prefix meaning “not” (with sandhi doubling of the following p).

appossukkatāya.gram: feminine abstract noun, instrumental/dative singular (appa + ussukka + -tā)
breakdown: Little, few (appa + u- coalesces to appo-).
Abstract noun suffix -tā in the instrumental/dative singular.

appātaṅko.meaning: having little sickness; free from affliction

appāyukasaṁvattanikā.meaning: conducive to a short life span
appāyukasaṁvattanikā.summary: This is a compound meaning “leading to short life”.
breakdown: Having a life span; from āyu “life”.

apuññāyatanaṁ.meaning: occasion of demerit; sphere of demerit
breakdown: Sphere, occasion, base (puñña + āyatana coalesces to puññāyatana).

apārutagharā.meaning: with open house; whose house stands open
breakdown: Open, opened; past participle of apāpurati “to open”.
Root “to cover, obstruct”; the negated-opening sense gives “opened, uncovered”.

apārā.meaning: not beyond; this side, the near shore

apāya.meaning: state of woe; lower world of existence; loss, ruin
breakdown: Root “to go”; literally “going away”, hence downfall, state of woe.
apāya.goenka: In the suttas apāya denotes the states of woe — the lower fields of existence into which beings arise after death through the ripening of unwholesome actions. Wrong view and unwholesome kamma are said to lead to apāya, duggati, vinipāta and niraya — states of woe, downfall, the lower fields and hell — while right view and wholesome conduct lead to happy states. The word also carries the everyday sense of loss or ruin, as in the causes of dissipating one's wealth.

apāyamukhāni.meaning: causes of ruin; ways of diminishing one’s wealth (literally “mouths of loss”)
breakdown: Mouths, openings; figuratively the channels through which wealth drains away.

apāyasahāyo.meaning: companion in ruin; friend who leads to loss

apāyaṁ.meaning: state of woe; going away, downfall (accusative)
apāyaṁ.gram: masculine noun, accusative singular of apāya
breakdown: Root “to go”; literally “going away”, hence state of woe.

apāyaṁ going.meaning: apāyaṁ: going away; state of woe
apāyaṁ going.gram: masculine noun, accusative singular of apāya
apāyaṁ going.note: uncertain: this headword is a glossary-row artifact; the entry is apāyaṁ with the gloss “going away, state of woe”. See apāyaṁ.

arahattaphalaṁ.meaning: the fruit of arahantship; the fruition of full liberation
breakdown: Arahantship; the state of an arahant, a fully liberated one.

arahaṁ.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular of arahant (irregular; from arahati “to be worthy”)
breakdown: Present participle stem; arahant is “one who is worthy”.
arahaṁ.goenka: Arahaṁ is the nominative singular of arahant, “a worthy one” — one of the foremost epithets of the Buddha and of every fully liberated being. An Arahant has walked the path to its end and eradicated all mental impurities; in the tradition such a one is also called khīṇāsavo, whose mind is freed from all four mental obsessions: desire for sensuality, desire for living, attachment to philosophical speculations, and ignorance.

arakajātakavaṇṇanā.meaning: the commentary on the Araka Jātaka, which relates that the Bodhisatta was once born as the brahmin Araka

arakasuttaṁ.meaning: the Araka Sutta, a discourse named after the teacher Araka
arakasuttaṁ.gram: proper noun, neuter (sutta title; Araka + suttaṁ)

araññasuttavaṇṇanā, sagāthāvagga-aṭṭhaka.meaning: the commentary on the Arañña Sutta, in the Sagāthāvagga commentary of the Saṁyutta Nikāya

araññasuttaṁ.meaning: the Arañña Sutta, the “Forest” discourse (arañña: forest, woods)

araññe.gram: neuter noun, locative singular of arañña

araññe in the.gram: neuter noun, locative singular of arañña
araññe in the.note: uncertain: this headword is a glossary-row artifact; the entry is araññe with the gloss “in the forest”. See araññe.

ariyamaggadhammaṁ.meaning: the Dhamma of the Noble Path; the teaching that is the noble path
ariyamaggadhammaṁ.gram: masculine/neuter noun, accusative singular, compound (ariya + magga + dhamma)
ariyamaggadhammaṁ.note: uncertain: the lexicon entry is fragmentary (“‘not this Dhamma’: is the noble path of”); the meaning is reconstructed from the compound members.

ariyamaggaphalasampayuttā.meaning: connected with the noble path and its fruit; resulting from the noble path and fruition
breakdown: Connected with, associated with; resulting from.

ariyamaggasamaṅgino.meaning: endowed with the Noble Path; possessing the noble path
ariyamaggasamaṅgino.gram: adjective, genitive singular (or nominative plural) of ariyamaggasamaṅgī, compound

ariyasaccakathā, mahāparinibbānasuttaṁ,.meaning: the “Discussion of the Noble Truths” section of the Mahāparinibbāna Sutta in the Dīgha Nikāya

ariyasaccānadassanaṁ.meaning: seeing the Noble Truths; witnessing the Noble Truths
breakdown: Of the truths (genitive plural saccānaṁ, shortened in the compound).

ariyāyatane.meaning: in the region of the Ariyas; in the sphere of the noble ones
ariyāyatane.gram: neuter noun, locative singular, compound (ariya + āyatana)
breakdown: Region, sphere, base; locative singular (ariya + āyatana coalesces to ariyāyatana).

asabbhā.meaning: low, vile; uncultured, not fit for the assembly
breakdown: Befitting the assembly (sabhā), cultured; hence asabbha “unworthy of decent society, vile”.

asaddhammappaṭisevanaṭṭhānavītikkamaceta.meaning: the volition of transgression consisting in indulging in unrighteous practice in a wrong place
asaddhammappaṭisevanaṭṭhānavītikkamaceta.summary: This is a long technical compound: unrighteous + practising + (wrong) place + transgression + volition.
breakdown: The untrue, unrighteous practice (a- “not” + saddhamma “true teaching/right conduct”).
Practising, indulging in (with sandhi doubling of the p).

asaddhammādhippāyena.meaning: with unrighteous intention; with a sinful wish
asaddhammādhippāyena.gram: masculine noun, instrumental singular, compound (asaddhamma + adhippāya)
breakdown: Sinful, unrighteous; the opposite of saddhamma, the true teaching.
With intention, with wish; instrumental singular of adhippāya.

asallīnaṁ.gram: past participle of na sallīyati (na + saṁ + √lī)
breakdown: Sluggish, cowering, shrunk back; saṁ + √lī “to cling, stick”.

asambhuṇanto.meaning: not being able; not adequate to
asambhuṇanto.gram: present participle, nominative singular masculine, of na sambhuṇāti
breakdown: Being able, being adequate; present participle of sambhuṇāti/sambhavati.
Obstruction, crowding, confinement; from saṁ + √bādh “to oppress”.

asammosāya.meaning: for the absence of confusion; for non-bewilderment
asammosāya.gram: masculine noun, dative singular of asammosa (na + saṁ + √mus)

asammuṭṭhā.meaning: not originating, not rising; not forgotten, unconfused
asammuṭṭhā.gram: past participle of na sammussati (na + saṁ + √mus)
breakdown: Forgotten, confused; past participle of sammussati.
asammuṭṭhā.note: divergence: the lexicon glosses “not + originating, rising”; DPD analyses the form as the past participle of na sammussati, “not forgotten”. The lexicon gloss is given first.

asammūḷho.gram: past participle, nominative singular masculine, of na sammuyhati (na + saṁ + √muh)
breakdown: Bewildered, infatuated; past participle of sammuyhati (mūḍha > mūḷha).

asamodhānaṁ.meaning: non-combination; absence of collocation or conjunction
breakdown: Collocation, combination, putting together; from saṁ + ava + √dhā.

asampakampiyo.gram: potential participle, nominative singular masculine, of na sampakampati (na + saṁ + pa + √kamp)
breakdown: To be shaken; potential participle of sampakampati “to shake, tremble”.
Conducive to, leading to, proceeding towards.
Meeting, union, coming together; from saṁ + ā + √gam “to go”.
